name: node-cacache
title: node-cacache
summary: fast, fault-tolerant, disk-based, data-agnostic, content-addressable cache
description:
fast, fault-tolerant, disk-based, data-agnostic, content-addressable cache
This module is a Node.js library for managing local key and content address
caches. It's really fast, really good at concurrency, and it will never give
you corrupted data, even if cache files get corrupted or manipulated.
.
It was originally written to be used as npm's local cache, but can just as
easily be used on its own.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-cacache because fast, fault-tolerant, disk-based, data-agnostic, content-addressable cache.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-cacache as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-cacache with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-cacache --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
